form_semantics.justification: Every input has a visible label, each step is wrapped in a fieldset/legend, email demonstrates aria-invalid='true' + aria-describedby with an actionable error message, correct input types (email, tel) and autocomplete attributes (cc-number, cc-exp, cc-csc, postal-code, etc.) are used throughout, required fields are indicated with both the required attribute and a visual asterisk, and no placeholder substitutes for a label.

heading_hierarchy.justification: Single h1 'Checkout' is correct, and the h1→h2 jump to 'Order summary' inside step 3 is valid; however only step 3 has an explicit heading inside its fieldset while steps 1 and 2 rely solely on their legend elements, creating a slightly inconsistent document outline.

element_choice.justification: All interactive elements are native (button, input, select); button[type=button] is used for step navigation and button[type=submit] for final submission; the progress indicator is an ol inside nav; the order summary uses a fully structured table — every element is the correct semantic match.

aria_discipline.justification: ARIA is used only where needed: aria-current='step' on the active step li, aria-invalid + aria-describedby on the erroneous email field, aria-hidden on decorative asterisks, and aria-label on the checkout steps nav; no redundant role or aria attributes duplicate native semantics.

content_realism.justification: Product names ('Wireless Bluetooth Headphones', 'USB-C Charging Cable (2-pack)') and prices ($79.99, $15.98, $95.97) are realistic and varied; a plausible set of countries is provided; the shop name, copyright year, and form action path are all believable.

landmark_structure.justification: Skip link targets main, header/main/footer landmarks are all present and correctly scoped, the checkout-steps nav is labelled with aria-label, and tabindex='-1' on main supports programmatic focus from the skip link; no landmarks are overused.

list_semantics.justification: The progress indicator correctly uses an ol (ordered, because step sequence matters) inside a nav; no other list-worthy collections are present.

table_semantics.justification: The order summary table has a caption, thead/tbody/tfoot, th scope='col' for column headers, and th scope='row' colspan='2' for the total row — full correct structure for two-dimensional tabular data.

expectations_met: Each step uses <fieldset> with <legend> for grouping
All inputs have associated <label> elements (not placeholder-only)
Email error uses aria-invalid='true' and aria-describedby pointing to error message
Error message text is actionable ('Enter a valid email address, like name@example.com')
Progress indicator conveys current step accessibly via aria-current='step' within a labelled <nav>
Card number, expiry, CVV fields have appropriate autocomplete attributes (cc-number, cc-exp, cc-csc)
Order summary uses a <table> with caption, thead, tbody, tfoot, and scoped th elements
Submit/continue buttons are <button> elements with clear, descriptive labels
No placeholder text used as a substitute for labels
Required fields indicated via required attribute and visible asterisk (not colour alone)
expectations_missed: Steps 1 and 2 lack explicit heading elements inside their fieldsets — only step 3 has an <h2>; a consistent approach (either headings inside all steps or none) would be cleaner, though the current structure is not technically invalid
additional_observations: tabindex='-1' on <main> is a valid pattern to support skip-link programmatic focus
The legend for step 1 includes visible '* required fields' text while the asterisk itself is aria-hidden, correctly pairing visual and programmatic required indication
card-expiry uses placeholder='MM/YY' as supplemental format hint alongside a proper label — acceptable usage
